name: Solace PubSub+ Event Broker API
what_it_does: Enterprise event broker and messaging platform with REST API and multiple protocol support — enabling organizations to implement event-driven architectures with high-throughput, low-latency messaging via AMQP 1.0, MQTT, JMS, WebSocket, and REST, with Solace PubSub+ Cloud for managed SaaS and on-premises hardware/software brokers.
use_cases: Implementing event-driven microservices via Solace PubSub+ API for high-throughput messaging
Publishing and subscribing to events via Solace REST API for multi-protocol message exchange
Managing event broker topology via Solace SEMP API for infrastructure-as-code configuration
Integrating IoT devices via Solace MQTT for real-time sensor data streaming
Building event mesh architectures via Solace for distributed multi-cloud event routing
not_for: Simple queue-based messaging (use AWS SQS or RabbitMQ for simpler message queuing)
Log streaming (use Kafka/Confluent for log aggregation at scale)
Small applications without enterprise messaging requirements
best_when: A large enterprise needs high-performance, multi-protocol event messaging (AMQP, MQTT, JMS, REST) with guaranteed delivery across hybrid cloud and on-premises environments — especially financial services and manufacturing.
avoid_when: You need simple message queuing, log streaming, or open-source without vendor licensing costs.

auth.notes: Multiple auth methods: (1) Basic auth (username:password) for broker access. (2) OAuth2/OIDC for PubSub+ Cloud management API. (3) API token for SEMP management API. SEMP (Solace Element Management Protocol): REST API for broker configuration — admin:password. REST messaging API: basic auth or client certificate. PubSub+ Cloud: OAuth2 with cloud.solace.com IdP. Protocols: AMQP 1.0, MQTT 3.1/5.0, JMS, WebSocket, REST. Client certificate authentication available for mTLS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: PubSub+ Standard (software): free developer edition; Community edition
pricing.paid_tiers: PubSub+ Enterprise: custom; PubSub+ Cloud: $250+/month; Hardware appliance: $50k+
pricing.notes: Canadian company (Ottawa). Founded 2001. Solace Systems → Solace Corporation (rebranded 2017). Private company. Originally hardware-based (specialized messaging appliances). Products: PubSub+ Event Broker (software), PubSub+ Hardware Appliance, PubSub+ Cloud (SaaS). Event Mesh: multi-broker distributed topology. SEMP: REST API for broker management/config. Protocol support: REST, AMQP 1.0, MQTT 3.1/5.0, JMS, WebSocket, SMF (native Solace protocol). Clients: Java, C, .NET, Python, Go, Node.js. Used by: financial services (capital markets), automotive (IoT), healthcare. Pub/sub with queuing: durable queues + topic subscriptions. High performance: millions of msgs/second.

performance.rate_limits: Hardware appliance: up to 100M msgs/second; Cloud: tier-based

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Multiple APIs: SEMP (management REST), REST messaging, native protocol SDKs. Multiple auth methods. Canadian company. Enterprise pricing. Hardware appliance option. Financial services focus. Multi-protocol (AMQP/MQTT/JMS/REST). PubSub+ Cloud vs self-hosted.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SEMP management API: idempotent by resource name. Message delivery: at-least-once, exactly-once configurable. Queue operations: idempotent queue creation by name. REST messaging: standard HTTP idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Multiple API layers: SEMP (management) vs REST messaging (data plane) — different auth and endpoints
SEMP API: admin credentials for broker configuration — high blast radius; restrict access
Protocol choice: REST API has higher overhead than native Solace SDK — use SDK for performance
PubSub+ Cloud vs self-hosted: different authentication paths (OAuth2 vs Basic auth)
Topic hierarchy: Solace uses '/' separator with wildcards (* and >) — different from MQTT '#' semantics
Hardware appliance: legacy hardware products exist — software PubSub+ is the modern path for new deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. mTLS client certificate support for strong mutual authentication. OAuth2/OIDC for cloud management. SOC2/ISO27001/PCI-DSS certified. SEMP admin API: high-privilege — restrict network access to management networks. Financial services heritage: strong security culture. Hardware appliance: FIPS 140-2 certified options. Message encryption: TLS in transit + optional application-layer encryption. Canadian company: GDPR compliance via EU endpoints.
